terms.notes: Second non-consecutive term. State government dismissed on 30 January 1991 by the Union government invoking Article 356 (largely on law-and-order grounds following LTTE-related concerns); replaced by President's Rule until the June 1991 election.
Imposed by the Chandra Shekhar government; lifted on swearing-in of the Jayalalithaa ministry after the June 1991 assembly election.

references.note: List of President's Rule impositions in Tamil Nadu.

terms.notes: First term. Won the 1991 assembly election in pre-poll alliance with the Indian National Congress; AIADMK secured 164/234 seats, INC 60.

Third term. Won the 1996 assembly election in pre-poll alliance with Tamil Maanila Congress (TMC). DMK formally joined the NDA at the national level in 1999 and remained until 2003; the state-level alliance with TMC continued through most of the term.

Second term. Won the 2001 election with INC as junior alliance partner. Disqualified by the Supreme Court on 21 September 2001 for conviction in the TANSI case; succeeded by O. Panneerselvam pending acquittal.
First stint as CM. Acting head of government keeping the seat for Jayalalithaa; no fresh election. Vacated when Jayalalithaa was acquitted and re-sworn-in.

references.note: Three stints as caretaker CM under AIADMK.

terms.notes: Third term (resumed). AIADMK-INC alliance fragmented over the term; AIADMK formally aligned with NDA at the centre in 2004 (lost all 39 Lok Sabha seats in TN that year).
Fourth term. DMK won the 2006 assembly election in pre-poll alliance with INC; the same DMK+INC bloc formed part of UPA-II at the centre after the 2009 LS election.
Fourth term. AIADMK-led front swept 2011 (203/234 seats). No formal pre-poll alliance with INC or BJP. Convicted in disproportionate-assets case on 27 September 2014; disqualified.
Second stint as CM, again as Jayalalithaa's caretaker. Vacated when she was acquitted by the Karnataka High Court and sworn in for a fifth term.
Fifth term (continuation of the 2011 mandate after acquittal). Term ended with the swearing-in of the new ministry post the May 2016 election; Jayalalithaa returned to power.
Sixth and final term. AIADMK won the 2016 election with 134/234 seats, the first incumbent return since 1989. Died in office on 5 December 2016 after extended hospitalisation.
Third stint as CM, following Jayalalithaa's death. Resigned amid the AIADMK leadership succession crisis pitting him against V. K. Sasikala and the Palaniswami faction.

Single term. AIADMK formally entered the National Democratic Alliance with BJP for the 2019 Lok Sabha election (TN allotted 39 LS seats; AIADMK contested 20). Continued in the state-level NDA bloc through the 2021 assembly poll.

First term. DMK won 133/234 seats in the 2021 election in pre-poll alliance with INC, VCK, CPI, CPI(M), MDMK and IUML (state-level brand: Secular Progressive Alliance). At the national level the DMK joined the INDIA bloc when it formed in July 2023. Term ended with the swearing-in of the post-May-2026 ministry.

Placeholder for the post-May-2026 government. The May 2026 results are ingested in this repository (datasets/elections/AcGenMay2026/S22/) but the formal swearing-in and final cabinet composition will be backfilled in a subsequent commit once the new ministry is sworn in. Downstream UI MUST treat null cm_name + null party_code as 'transition state' and render the neutral 'pending' colour, not a party colour.

references.note: 2026 partywise result; ministry not yet sworn in at file creation time.
